findings.problem: The narration repeatedly breaks into clipped noun-phrase fragments at emotional peaks. That violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration and starts to feel like shorthand pressure rather than earned rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the fragment clusters beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:127, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:515, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:643, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:687, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:693 into grammatically complete sentences. Keep the urgency, but carry it through syntax, punctuation, and sequencing instead of standalone labels or subjectless shards.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised fragment clusters beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:127, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:515, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:643, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:687, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:693 read as complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs; FAIL if any of those spans still rely on bare time markers, noun phrases, or one-word labels as standalone narrative sentences.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same emotional-impact template too many times: words, admissions, lines, and even a number all 'land,' 'hit,' or 'strike.' After the third recurrence it reads like autopilot rather than precise perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the emotional beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:423, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:491, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:589 so each moment earns its own bodily or psychological texture instead of recycling the same impact verb logic.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised beats beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:423, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:491, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:589 no longer leave three or more instances of abstract things 'landing,' 'hitting,' or 'striking' Tiana or the scene; FAIL if three or more of those cited beats still use that same construction family.

findings.problem: The Malik parking-lot confrontation explains the chapter's thesis after the props and missed deadline have already made it legible. Malik states the same grievance in several consecutive formulations, then the narration restates the institutional point, which thins subtext at the chapter's climactic turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the argumentative stretch beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:579, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:587, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:593, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:607. Let the packed trunk, gas receipt, cancelled email, and Tiana's reaction carry more of the meaning so Malik sets one sharp boundary instead of reiterating the same thesis three ways.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised parking-lot argument in the span beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:579 makes Malik's boundary clear with one primary articulation and lets Tiana's response plus the physical props carry the rest; FAIL if the scene still contains multiple consecutive speeches restating that she treats Malik as the part that can wait.

summary: Strong causal chapter with clean continuity, distinct dialogue, and no sanitization drift, but it does not clear pass-level on Booker/prose because the narration leans on constitution-banned clipped fragments and a repeated impact formula, and the Malik confrontation overexplains a conflict the scene already proves.
